req.title: Hr-Admin creates policy-document
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to create a policy-document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for HR Admin Policy Document Creation requirement.

Requirement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to create a policy-document
through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record
must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timezone


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def hr_admin_user():
    """A fully authenticated HR-admin user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-001"
    user.username = "hr_admin_jane"
    user.email = "jane@example.com"
    user.roles = ["hr-admin"]
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_hr_admin_user():
    """An authenticated user who does NOT hold the hr-admin role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-002"
    user.username = "regular_employee"
    user.email = "bob@example.com"
    user.roles = ["employee"]
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """An unauthenticated (anonymous) user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    user.roles = []
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def valid_policy_form_data():
    """Minimal valid payload covering all mandatory fields."""
    return {
        "title": "Remote Work Policy",
        "category": "HR",
        "effective_date": "2024-06-01",
        "version": "1.0",
        "content": "This policy governs remote working arrangements.",
        "owner_department": "Human Resources",
        "status": "draft",
    }


@pytest.fixture
def policy_document_service(mocker):
    """Patched PolicyDocumentService used by the controller/view."""
    service = mocker.patch(
        "app.services.policy_document_service.PolicyDocumentService",
        autospec=True,
    )
    return service.return_value


@pytest.fixture
def audit_service(mocker):
    """Patched AuditService."""
    service = mocker.patch(
        "app.services.audit_service.AuditService",
        autospec=True,
    )
    return service.return_value


@pytest.fixture
def client(app, hr_admin_user):
    """
    Flask/FastAPI test client pre-logged-in as hr_admin_user.
    Adjust the login helper to match your actual auth mechanism.
    """
    from app import create_app  # noqa: PLC0415  (project-specific import)

    application = create_app(testing=True)
    with application.test_client() as c:
        with application.app_context():
            # Simulate session-based login
            with c.session_transaction() as sess:
                sess["user_id"] = hr_admin_user.id
                sess["roles"] = hr_admin_user.roles
        yield c


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper / shared constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

MANDATORY_FIELDS = [
    "title",
    "category",
    "effective_date",
    "version",
    "content",
    "owner_department",
    "status",
]

CREATE_POLICY_URL = "/policy-documents/create"


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Authentication & Authorisation
# ===========================================================================

class TestAccessControl:
    """Only authenticated hr-admins may reach the creation form/endpoint."""

    def test_hr_admin_can_access_create_form(self, client):
        """GET /policy-documents/create returns 200 for hr-admin."""
        response = client.get(CREATE_POLICY_URL)
        assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_unauthenticated_user_redirected_to_login(self, client, mocker):
        """Unauthenticated request must be redirected (302) to the login page."""
        mocker.patch("app.auth.get_current_user", return_value=None)
        response = client.get(CREATE_POLICY_URL, follow_redirects=False)
        assert response.status_code in (302, 401)
        if response.status_code == 302:
            assert "/login" in response.headers["Location"]

    def test_non_hr_admin_role_is_forbidden(self, client, mocker, non_hr_admin_user):
        """A user without hr-admin role receives 403 Forbidden."""
        mocker.patch("app.auth.get_current_user", return_value=non_hr_admin_user)
        response = client.get(CREATE_POLICY_URL)
        assert response.status_code == 403

    def test_expired_session_cannot_create_policy(self, client, mocker):
        """An expired/invalidated session is treated as unauthenticated."""
        mocker.patch("app.auth.get_current_user", return_value=None)
        response = client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data={"title": "Test"},
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        assert response.status_code in (302, 401)


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Successful Policy Document Creation
# ===========================================================================

class TestSuccessfulPolicyCreation:
    """Happy-path scenarios for policy document creation."""

    def test_post_valid_form_returns_success(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """Submitting a fully populated valid form results in HTTP 201/302."""
        policy_document_service.create.return_value = MagicMock(
            id="doc-999", **valid_policy_form_data
        )
        response = client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=valid_policy_form_data,
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        assert response.status_code in (201, 302)

    def test_created_document_persisted_with_correct_fields(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """Service layer receives exactly the data submitted in the form."""
        policy_document_service.create.return_value = MagicMock(id="doc-999")
        client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=valid_policy_form_data,
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        call_kwargs = policy_document_service.create.call_args
        assert call_kwargs is not None, "PolicyDocumentService.create was not called"
        submitted = call_kwargs.kwargs if call_kwargs.kwargs else call_kwargs.args[0]
        for field, value in valid_policy_form_data.items():
            assert submitted[field] == value, (
                f"Field '{field}': expected '{value}', got '{submitted[field]}'"
            )

    def test_success_redirects_to_document_detail(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """On success the user is redirected to the new document's detail page."""
        new_doc_id = "doc-999"
        policy_document_service.create.return_value = MagicMock(id=new_doc_id)
        response = client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=valid_policy_form_data,
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        assert response.status_code == 302
        assert new_doc_id in response.headers["Location"]

    def test_success_flash_message_displayed(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """A success flash/notification message is present in the response."""
        policy_document_service.create.return_value = MagicMock(id="doc-999")
        response = client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=valid_policy_form_data,
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        assert b"successfully created" in response.data.lower()

    def test_creator_id_stored_on_document(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The document must record which user created it."""
        policy_document_service.create.return_value = MagicMock(id="doc-999")
        client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=valid_policy_form_data,
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        call_kwargs = policy_document_service.create.call_args
        submitted = call_kwargs.kwargs if call_kwargs.kwargs else call_kwargs.args[0]
        assert submitted.get("created_by") == hr_admin_user.id


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Audit Record on Successful Creation
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditRecord:
    """Verify audit trail is written after a successful document creation."""

    def test_audit_record_written_on_success(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """AuditService.log is called exactly once after successful creation."""
        new_doc_id = "doc-999"
        policy_document_service.create.return_value = MagicMock(id=new_doc_id)
        client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=valid_policy_form_data,
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        audit_service.log.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_correct_action(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
        audit_service,
        hr_admin_user,
    ):
        """Audit entry action field must be 'CREATE_POLICY_DOCUMENT'."""
        policy_document_service.create.return_value = MagicMock(id="doc-999")
        client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=valid_policy_form_data,
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        log_call = audit_service.log.call_args
        assert log_call is not None
        payload = log_call.kwargs if log_call.kwargs else log_call.args[0]
        assert payload["action"] == "CREATE_POLICY_DOCUMENT"

    def test_audit_record_contains_actor_id(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
        audit_service,
        hr_admin_user,
    ):
        """Audit entry must reference the hr-admin's user ID as the actor."""
        policy_document_service.create.return_value = MagicMock(id="doc-999")
        client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=valid_policy_form_data,
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        log_call = audit_service.log.call_args
        payload = log_call.kwargs if log_call.kwargs else log_call.args[0]
        assert payload["actor_id"] == hr_admin_user.id

    def test_audit_record_contains_document_id(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit entry must reference the ID of the newly created document."""
        new_doc_id = "doc-999"
        policy_document_service.create.return_value = MagicMock(id=new_doc_id)
        client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=valid_policy_form_data,
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        log_call = audit_service.log.call_args
        payload = log_call.kwargs if log_call.kwargs else log_call.args[0]
        assert payload["resource_id"] == new_doc_id

    def test_audit_record_has_timestamp(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit entry must contain an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp."""
        policy_document_service.create.return_value = MagicMock(id="doc-999")
        client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=valid_policy_form_data,
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        log_call = audit_service.log.call_args
        payload = log_call.kwargs if log_call.kwargs else log_call.args[0]
        ts = payload.get("timestamp")
        assert ts is not None, "Audit record must contain a 'timestamp' field"
        # Accept datetime objects or ISO-8601 strings
        if isinstance(ts, str):
            parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(ts)
            assert parsed.tzinfo is not None, "Timestamp must be timezone-aware"
        else:
            assert isinstance(ts, datetime)

    def test_audit_record_not_written_on_validation_failure(
        self,
        client,
        policy_document_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """If the form fails validation, NO audit record should be written."""
        response = client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data={"title": ""},          # missing / blank mandatory fields
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        audit_service.log.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_record_not_written_on_service_exception(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """If the persistence layer raises, audit must NOT be written."""
        policy_document_service.create.side_effect = RuntimeError("DB unavailable")
        client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=valid_policy_form_data,
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        audit_service.log.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Mandatory Field Validation
# ===========================================================================

class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:
    """Each mandatory field, when absent or blank, must fail validation."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_rejected(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
        audit_service,
        missing_field,
    ):
        """Removing any single mandatory field must produce a 400/422 or re-render the form."""
        incomplete_data = {k: v for k, v in valid_policy_form_data.items() if k != missing_field}
        response = client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=incomplete_data,
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        assert response.status_code in (200, 400, 422), (
            f"Expected form re-render or client error when '{missing_field}' is absent, "
            f"got {response.status_code}"
        )
        policy_document_service.create.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("blank_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_blank_mandatory_field_rejected(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
        audit_service,
        blank_field,
    ):
        """Setting any mandatory field to an empty string must fail validation."""
        bad_data = dict(valid_policy_form_data)
        bad_data[blank_field] = ""
        response = client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=bad_data,
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        assert response.status_code in (200, 400, 422)
        policy_document_service.create.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("blank_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_validation_error_message_shown_for_blank_field(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        blank_field,
    ):
        """The response body must contain a field-level error message for the blank field."""
        bad_data = dict(valid_policy_form_data)
        bad_data[blank_field] = ""
        response = client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=bad_data,
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        body = response.data.decode()
        assert (
            blank_field.replace("_", " ") in body.lower()
            or "required" in body.lower()
            or "field" in body.lower()
        ), f"Expected validation message referencing '{blank_field}' in response body"

    def test_whitespace_only_title_rejected(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
    ):
        """A title consisting solely of whitespace must be treated as blank."""
        bad_data = dict(valid_policy_form_data)
        bad_data["title"] = "     "
        response = client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=bad_data,
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        assert response.status_code in (200, 400, 422)
        policy_document_service.create.assert_not_called()

    def test_invalid_effective_date_format_rejected(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
        policy_document_service,
    ):
        """effective_date must be a valid date; garbage input is rejected."""
        bad_data = dict(valid_policy_form_data)
        bad_data["effective_date"] = "not-a-date"
        response = client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=bad_data,
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        assert response.status_code in (200, 400, 422)
        policy_document_service.create.assert_not_called()

    def test_all_fields_blank_returns_all_errors(
        self,
        client,
        policy_document_service,
    ):
        """Submitting a completely empty form must surface errors for all mandatory fields."""
        empty_data = {field: "" for field in MANDATORY_FIELDS}
        response = client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=empty_data,
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        assert response.status_code in (200, 400, 422)
        body = response.data.decode().lower()
        # At minimum the word 'required' or 'error' must appear
        assert "required" in body or "error" in body
        policy_document_service.create.assert_not_called()

    def test_form_repopulates_on_validation_failure(
        self,
        client,
        valid_policy_form_data,
    ):
        """Previously entered values must be preserved so the user need not retype them."""
        bad_data = dict(valid_policy_form_data)
        bad_data["title"] = ""             # trigger an error
        response = client.post(
            CREATE_POLICY_URL,
            data=bad_data,
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        body = response.data.decode()
        # The valid category value should still be present in the re-rendered form
        assert valid_policy_form_data["category"] in body


# ===========================================================================
# 5. UI / Form rendering
# ===========================================================================

class TestFormRendering:
    """Verify the creation form itself is rendered correctly for hr-admins."""

    def test_create_form_renders_all_mandatory_fields(self, client):
        """GET request returns a form containing all mandatory input elements."""
        response = client.get(CREATE_POLICY_URL)
        body = response.data.decode()
        for field in MANDATORY_FIELDS:
            assert field in body, (
                f"Mandatory field '{field}' not found in create-policy form HTML"
            )

    def test_create_form_contains_submit_button(self, client):
        """The form must include a submit / save button."""
        response = client.get(CREATE_POLICY_URL)
        body = response.data.decode().lower()
        assert 'type="submit"' in body or "submit" in body

    def test_create_form_uses_post_method(self, client):
        """The HTML form must use HTTP POST."""
        response = client.get(CREATE_POLICY_URL)
        body = response.data.decode().lower()
        assert 'method="post"' in body or "method='post'" in body

    def test_csrf_token_present_in_form(self, client):
        """A CSRF token input must be rendered to protect the form."""
        response = client.get(CREATE_POLICY_URL)
        body = response.data.decode().lower()
        assert "csrf" in body, "CSRF token must be present in the create-policy form"
```

---

### Key design decisions

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Fixtures** | Separate fixtures for `hr_admin_user`, `non_hr_admin_user`, and `unauthenticated_user` so every auth scenario is self-contained. |
| **Patching** | `policy_document_service` and `audit_service` are mocked so tests are unit-level fast and don't need a real DB. |
| **Parametrize** | `@pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)` generates one test per mandatory field, ensuring none are accidentally skipped. |
| **Audit assertions** | Positive cases check `assert_called_once()`; negative cases (validation failure, service exception) check `assert_not_called()` — both sides of the contract. |
| **Status codes** | Ranges (`in (200, 400, 422)`) are used for validation tests so the suite works with both Flask (400) and FastAPI/Pydantic (422) backends with minimal change. |
| **Test classes** | Grouped by concern (access control, success, audit, validation, rendering) for readability and selective execution (`pytest -k TestAuditRecord`). |

class TestAccessControl:

    def test_hr_admin_can_access_create_form(self, client):
        """GET /policy-documents/create returns 200 for hr-admin."""
        response = client.get(CREATE_POLICY_URL)
        assert response.status_code == 200

        """Unauthenticated request must be redirected (302) to the login page."""

        response = cl
